name: Solidus Open-Source E-Commerce API
what_it_does: Open-source Ruby on Rails e-commerce platform with REST API — enabling development teams to build highly customized online stores with products, variants, orders, customers, payments, shipping, and promotions, with headless commerce capabilities via REST API, forked from Spree and designed for enterprise customization without compromise.
use_cases: Managing Solidus products and variants via API for catalog automation and PIM integration
Processing Solidus orders and payments via API for order management workflow automation
Managing Solidus customer accounts and addresses via API for CRM integration
Configuring Solidus shipping and promotion rules via API for business logic automation
Building headless Solidus storefront via API for decoupled frontend React/Vue commerce
not_for: Teams without Ruby on Rails expertise (Solidus is a Rails engine — requires Ruby)
Non-technical merchants needing hosted SaaS e-commerce (use Shopify or BigCommerce)
Simple stores without complex customization needs (use WooCommerce or Medusa)
best_when: A Ruby on Rails development team or agency needs a fully customizable open-source e-commerce platform — where Solidus's extension architecture allows unlimited business logic customization without fighting the framework.
avoid_when: You don't have Ruby/Rails expertise, want managed SaaS, or need a simpler platform without Rails complexity.

auth.notes: Bearer token auth via Doorkeeper (OAuth2 gem). API key (token) for API access. OAuth2 client credentials or password flow. Admin API vs storefront API tokens. Webhook via Solidus Webhooks extension. No fine-grained scopes in core — extension-based. Rails DeviseTokenAuth for alternative auth. Admin token vs user token separation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (BSD-3-Clause license)
pricing.paid_tiers: Solidus Starter (community); Solidus Enterprise (Nebulab support) custom
pricing.notes: Open source project. Created by Bonobos (fashion brand) in 2015, forked from Spree. BSD-3-Clause license. GitHub: solidusio/solidus (4,700+ stars). Ruby on Rails engine. Maintained by Nebulab (Italian Rails consultancy). Extension ecosystem (100+ extensions). Used by: Bonobos, Casper, Huckberry, Ministry of Supply. Strong in DTC (direct-to-consumer) brands. Headless commerce via Solidus Headless extension. Payment processing via multiple gateways. Multi-currency, multi-language. B2B extensions available. Active US and European community.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with OAuth2 bearer tokens. Webhook via extension. PCI-DSS compliance via payment gateway extensions. Ruby/Rails expertise required. Self-hosted. No rate limits from platform. BSD-3-Clause license. Extension architecture may require custom API endpoints. Admin vs storefront token separation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product reads idempotent by ID or slug. Order reads idempotent by number. Customer reads idempotent. Order creation not idempotent — use order token pattern. Payment capture idempotent for same transaction.
known_agent_gotchas: Ruby on Rails expertise required — not for non-Ruby developers
Extension ecosystem — headless, webhooks require separate extensions installation
OAuth2 via Doorkeeper — standard but requires proper token management
Admin API vs storefront API have different permissions and endpoints
PCI-DSS compliance requires careful payment handling — use tokenized payments only

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (Rails default). User-controlled data residency. OAuth2 via Doorkeeper. PCI-DSS compliance for payment handling. Admin vs user token separation. Rails CSRF and XSS protection built-in. Open source — auditable. Payment tokenization required for PCI compliance. Extension security depends on extension quality.
